camps.name: Bahari Beach Hotel
camps.description: Sustainable tourism needs are met through solar power, emphasizing the camp’s commitment to renewable energy.

camps.name: Diani Reef Resort and Spa
camps.description: Diani Reef’s commitment extends to local community empowerment and cultural preservation, while maintaining high standards in environmental management, staff welfare, and visitor education, embodying a holistic approach to eco-friendly hospitality.

camps.name: Severin Safari Camp
camps.description: Focusing on sustainability through environmental audits, waste management, water conservation, and energy efficiency. The resort actively supports local communities and wildlife conservation.

camps.name: Afro Chic Diani
camps.description: Sustainability through environmental audits, efficient waste management, and water conservation practices. It emphasizes energy efficiency, pollution control, and community engagement, supporting local initiatives and cultural preservation.

camps.name: Voyager Ziwani Tented Camp
camps.description: Emphasizing a low ecological footprint, the camp supports local wildlife monitoring and boasts a successful tree planting initiative. Innovative waste water treatment ensures recycling and reuse, complementing solid waste management and pollution control measures.

camps.name: The Sands at Nomad
camps.description: Sustainable tourism needs are met through solar power, emphasizing the camp’s commitment to renewable energy.

camps.name: Voyager Beach Resort
camps.description: On top of planting over 1,100 trees and educating local children on conservation, the camp’s innovative waste management, utilizes biodigesters and composting, complementing its efficient water and energy use, with solar panels and LPG cooking.

camps.name: Serena Beach Resort and Spa
camps.description: Serena Hotels has been a leader in sustainable tourism for over 40 years. Committed to combining developmental objectives with economic sustainability, the company focuses on best practices in social, cultural, environmental, and economic development. Through responsible business practices, eco-friendly measures, and community engagement, Serena Hotels aligns with the Sustainable Development Goals to make a positive impact on the environment, economy, and society.

camps.name: Medina Palms
camps.description: Medina Palms has invested in an organic recycling and composite system, that manages waste from the kitchen and is reused in the vegetable gardens as manure.

camps.name: Ol Pejeta Bush Camp
camps.description: Sustainable tourism needs at Ol Pejeta Bush Camp are met through solar power, emphasizing the camp’s commitment to renewable energy. They also play a big part in helping with the sustainable efforts of the conservancy.

camps.name: Kifaru House Lewa
camps.description: We’re deeply committed to eco-friendly practices and wildlife conservation. From minimizing our environmental footprint to supporting local communities, our efforts extend across our entire operation. Our conservation initiatives include protecting endangered species such as rhinos and Grevy zebras, while our outreach programs focus on healthcare, education, micro-finance, and water projects for nearby communities.

camps.name: Loisaba Star Beds
camps.description: As part of the Loisaba Community Trust, our 57,000-acre conservancy is dedicated to wildlife conservation and community improvement, ensuring a sustainable future for all.  The Loisaba mission is to protect and enhance critical wildlife diversity, abundance and habitat in the Loisaba landscape, which sits on the western edge of one of Kenya’s most important elephant movement corridors, while concurrently supporting sustainable livestock production and improving the lives of neighbouring communities.

camps.name: Sanctuary Olonana Camp
camps.description: The camp’s sustainable tourism needs are met through solar power, emphasizing the camp’s commitment to renewable energy.

camps.name: Naboisho Camp
camps.description: We’re committed to empowering vital wilderness areas in East Africa, fostering sustainability for both communities and ecosystems. Through strategic investments and collaboration with local partners, NGOs, and guests, we’re turning vulnerable regions into thriving conservation economies. With each guest’s contribution, we’re making significant strides towards our goal, ensuring a positive impact on both people and nature.

camps.name: Mara Plains Camp
camps.description: In our pursuit of sustainable conservation, we’ve witnessed the decline of precious natural habitats. To counteract this, we’ve pioneered bold conservation models that integrate communities, governments, and the private sector. Through low-impact tourism, we fund compassionate protection and intelligent management of stressed environments, ensuring benefits for both communities and wildlife.

camps.name: Kilima Camp
camps.description: Focusing on sustainability through environmental audits, waste management, water conservation, and energy efficiency. The resort actively supports local communities and wildlife conservation.

camps.name: Kicheche Valley Camp
camps.description: Sustainability through environmental audits, efficient waste management, and water conservation practices. It emphasizes energy efficiency, pollution control, and community engagement, supporting local initiatives and cultural preservation.

camps.name: Kicheche Mara Camp
camps.description: Emphasizing a low ecological footprint, the camp supports local wildlife monitoring and boasts a successful tree planting initiative. Innovative waste water treatment ensures recycling and reuse, complementing solid waste management and pollution control measures.

camps.name: Kicheche Bush Camp
camps.description: Sustainable tourism needs are met through solar power, emphasizing the camp’s commitment to renewable energy.

camps.name: Karen Blixen Camp
camps.description: On top of planting over 1,100 trees and educating local children on conservation, the camp’s innovative waste management, utilizes biodigesters and composting, complementing its efficient water and energy use, with solar panels and LPG cooking.

camps.name: Rekero Camp
camps.description: The hotel offers tourists a chance to experience wildlife and nature conservation firsthand. Their environmental efforts include an annual self-audit, the ‘Ocean Love Initiative’ for beach clean-ups, and support for the Kenya Wildlife Service’s clean-ups.

camps.name: Elephant Pepper Camp
camps.description: Amboseli Serena Safari Lodge  practices energy conservation, waste recycling, and sustainable sewage management. Its Environmental Management System (EMS) focuses on water, energy, and waste, with annual audits following local regulations.

camps.description: The camp’s sustainable tourism needs are met through solar power, emphasizing the camp’s commitment to renewable energy.

camps.name: Loldia House
camps.description: Loldia House supplies the bulk of meat required to feed rescued birds at both the Naivasha and Soysambu rehabilitation centres. 50 Kg of meat is given to vultures, and this lasts one week.

camps.name: Sarova Lion Hill Lodge
camps.description: In partnership with KWS, the camp conducts clean ups at the town centre. This is normally done along the route that leads to the park. The lodge donated gloves and water to be used by those that participated in the process.

camps.name: Lake Naivasha Country Club
camps.description: The facility carries out regular environmental clean-up activities from Karagita main road to the premises. Approximately over 100 indigenous tree species such as Acacia have been planted within the facility. The management also supports KWS rangers through provision of water.

camps.name: Lewa Safari Camp
camps.description: Emphasizing a low ecological footprint, the camp supports local wildlife monitoring and boasts a successful tree planting initiative. Innovative waste water treatment ensures recycling and reuse, complementing solid waste management and pollution control measures.

camps.name: Lake Elmenteita Serena Camp
camps.description: Emphasizing a low ecological footprint, the camp supports local wildlife monitoring and boasts a successful tree planting initiative. Innovative waste water treatment ensures recycling and reuse, complementing solid waste management and pollution control measures.

camps.name: Lake Nakuru Sopa Lodge
camps.description: The lodge forms part of the park stakeholders committee, which works together with Kenya Wildlife Service (KWS) in implementing conservation projects such as ‘Cycling with rhinos’ event which is held annually. The lodge participates in the planning, sensitization and execution of the project in conjunction with local schools.

camps.name: Great Rift Valley Lodge and Golf Resort
camps.description: The facility has tree planting activity conducted within the premises. Each staff has a set target of 10 trees to plant in every year. A list of tree species planted was verified. Seedlings are obtained from the facility’s own nursery bed.

camps.name: Lake Naivasha Sopa Resort
camps.description: The resort conducts low environmental impact activities such as bird watching, nature walks, cycling, boat rides and fishing activities for its visitors. The activities are aimed at sensitizing the visitors on the surrounding environment.

camps.name: Cottars 1920’s Camp
camps.description: Cottars Safari camp supports conservation initiatives through the Cottars Wildlife Conservation Trust (CWCT), which manages Olderkesi conservancy on behalf of the local Maasai community and the Olderkesi Community Wildlife Conservation Trust (OCWCT).
